steps: Open Claude Code in your newsroom project folder and ask it to create (or open) CLAUDE.md, with this as the very first rule: never accept unpublished investigative material or confidential source identities, in any form.
Tell Claude your house style — how you refer to the mayor's office, which sources need a second confirmation, banned words like 'slammed' — and have it write those rules into CLAUDE.md underneath.
Ask Claude to read the file back to you and confirm the no-go rule is still the first thing anyone sees when it opens the file.
Use Claude Code normally on a story that has no unpublished material or source names anywhere near it.
The next time an output comes back wrong — wrong style, wrong assumption — tell Claude to update CLAUDE.md itself so it doesn't repeat that mistake.
Ask it to show you exactly what changed in the file before you move on to the next story.
verify: Open CLAUDE.md and check that the unpublished-material rule is still the first rule in the file. If a later edit pushed it down or dropped it, put it back at the top before anyone uses this folder again.
